purpose: Ensures hci_cmd_sync_queue returns an error when HCI_UNREGISTER is set, preventing commands from being queued after the device is unregistered and avoiding a use‑after‑free.
Removes an unnecessary asm/termios.h include from n_hdlc.c, relying on linux/tty.h which already provides the needed termios definitions, simplifying the include hierarchy.
Updates perf c2c documentation to include the newly added 'N/A' store metric in the report table, reflecting a change in metric definitions.
Fixes race and double‑free bugs in hugetlb VMA lock handling during VMA split and range unmapping, and ensures a lock is allocated for all VM_MAYSHARE vmas.
Adds the AVDD and MICVDD power supply entries to the rt5682s device‑tree binding documentation, reflecting supplies already used in a board DTS.
Adds a missing iounmap() in zorro7xx_remove_one() to release a resource allocated by ioremap() during probe, fixing a leak.
Deletes the unused 'work' member from struct mlx5e_async_ctx in the mlx5e driver, eliminating dead code.
Changes cfg80211_auth_request.key_idx from unsigned to signed to allow -1 sentinel values and silence static‑analysis warnings; no functional behavior change.
The commit reorders the entries of CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in RISC-V defconfig files to match the ordering produced by savedefconfig, without altering any functional behavior.
The patch adds a helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to test whether the MCP251xfd CAN controller operates in CAN-FD mode and replaces existing inline checks with calls to this function.
The patch modifies the RTC self‑test suite to detect if a real‑time clock device is absent and, in that case, skip the test instead of marking it as failed.

The patch removes an unnecessary assignment to the variable 'bytes' in sound/pci/echoaudio/midi.c, which was overwritten later and never read, eliminating a dead store warning from clang static analysis.

The patch modifies kernel configuration to allow rcuscale to be built without automatically enabling RCU Tasks on PREEMPT_NONE kernels, removing an unnecessary dependency and reducing kernel size and tracing complexity.
The patch edits spte.h in the KVM x86 MMU code to delete a duplicated phrase "be split" in a comment or string, correcting it to a single occurrence.

The commit reverts a previous modification that filled the DCN315 clock table using dcfclk values. The revert is needed because when dcfclk states are disabled, the table leads to a no‑read/underflow condition, so the change restores the prior behavior until a proper fix is implemented.
The commit updates the device‑tree bindings documentation for the Mali‑Bifrost GPU on the Renesas RZ/V2L (R9A07G054) SoC by adding the appropriate compatible string entry.
The patch renames the struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp to align with the driver’s naming convention, updating all references accordingly.

The commit corrects a printf‑style format string in the AMD XGBE driver: it changes the %hx specifier to %x (or equivalent) so that the promoted int argument from ntohs() matches the format, eliminating a clang -Wformat warning.

The patch modifies the MIPS jump_label compatibility code to cast the upper bound of a branch range to a signed long before comparison, ensuring a correct signed check and preventing warnings from negative offsets.
The commit removes the HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case from SetHwReg8188EU() and calls the underlying function directly, simplifying the driver and eliminating an unnecessary HAL abstraction.

The patch adds the `static` qualifier to symbols in arch/arm64/kernel/cpu_errata.c that are only used within the file, silencing Sparse warnings about undeclared symbols and preventing them from being exported globally.
Removes an incorrect interrupt‑flags cell from the MSM display MDSS device‑tree bindings because the provider supplies only a single interrupt cell, correcting the binding specification.
The patch modifies the v4l2 control reference code to allocate memory for array members inside struct ctrl_ref, preparing the kernel to let drivers resize those arrays later.
The commit extends the Qualcomm socinfo driver by adding entries for additional SoCs (SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P, SM8450) and related PMICs, enabling the kernel to recognize these platforms.
The patch expands the rtl8xxxu Wi‑Fi driver’s chip‑cut handling to accept version letters A‑P, replacing the previous fallback that reported "unknown" for cuts beyond E.
The patch removes the .is_enabled flag from PRCMU clock drivers because the generic clock core already tracks enable counts, eliminating redundant state.

The patch reorganizes XIP‑related #ifdef blocks in the RISC‑V memory initialization code, moving the pt_ops macro next to its conditional and grouping similar XIP macros to reduce scattered CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL checks.
Enables QUP and GPI DMA nodes in the sm6350-lena device tree, preparing the board for future I2C functionality.
The commit updates comments in the rzg2l thermal driver: replaces an unclear label with a clearer one, corrects a spelling typo, and moves a block comment to its own line to follow coding style.
The patch adds handling for failure to acquire a hardware semaphore in release_swfw_sync, logging an error and exiting to avoid an infinite loop when hardware is surprise‑removed.
The commit updates the pcm3168a ASoC driver to replace legacy bus‑clock defines with the newer ASoC DAI format terminology, aligning the code with current naming conventions.
The patch deletes a conditional block in the NFS code that checks IS_SWAPFILE, because the macro is always defined, making the code dead. The change simply removes this unnecessary hack.
Implements handling of GTP_ECHO_REQ packets by sending a GTP_ECHO_RSP with the required IE, making the kernel GTP device conform to the GTP specification.
The patch replaces uses of cpumask_next_* with cpumask_first_* when the start index is -1 (i.e., beginning of mask) for efficiency, and introduces a missing cpumask_first_zero() helper.
The patch removes the driver’s call to the unsupported "dtim_assoc" IOVAR in brcmfmac, stopping repeated error messages after a Wi‑Fi roam because the firmware no longer provides TIM information.
Adds a new ASoC board configuration (adl_rt5682) for Intel platforms that lack a speaker amplifier, wiring the alc5682 headset codec to SSP0 per Intel BT offload design.

Adds a check in the io_uring networking path so that zerocopy sendmsg operations are rejected with an error when the underlying socket does not support zerocopy, mirroring earlier handling for zerocopy send.
Adds a conditional test for xeCJK.sty in the kerneldoc preamble, loading it only if the file exists, to avoid xelatex errors on systems where texlive-xecjk is not installed.
The patch modifies scripts/checkpatch.pl to stop flagging macros that contain a 'case' statement as errors, recognizing that such macros are used in drivers.

The patch switches simple‑KMS helper functions in the simpledrm driver to the standard DRM atomic helpers, removes empty CRTC enable/disable helpers, and always calls drm_atomic_helper_check_plane_state(). No runtime behavior changes, only internal refactoring for better architecture and code reuse.
Adds a JPEG decode worker interface in the Media driver to allow three hardware decoders to run in parallel on the MT8195 SoC.
The patch deletes VPRINTK debug prints in libata and removes the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() helper because tracepoints already provide the same information, cleaning up redundant code.

Adds capability for AMD DRM driver to enter low‑power zstate during the extra vertical blank period created by FreeSync video mode, calculating available time and adjusting registers, with restore on exit.

The commit updates the function pointer prototype for hqd_destroy() so its third parameter type matches the actual implementations (enum kfd_preempt_type), eliminating a kernel CFI type mismatch that caused a runtime failure on certain GPUs.
Updates the STM32 pinctrl device‑tree binding: renames the property from 'reset' to the correct 'resets' and adds 'additionalProperties: false' to the schema.

The patch modifies ath9k_hw_resettxqueue to silence a GCC‑12 array‑bounds warning about accessing txq[32] on a 10‑element array, likely via a cast, pragma, or index adjustment, without changing runtime behavior.
The patch updates the sun8i-ss crypto driver to replace pm_runtime_get_sync calls with pm_runtime_resume_and_get, using a more suitable runtime‑PM API and simplifying the code.

The patch moves the fixed‑file install/remove helpers into the filetable subsystem and changes their API to accept a context (ctx) instead of an io_kiocb, reflecting that the operation is not tied to a specific request.
Updates the device‑tree binding documentation for the NXP i.MX8MQ VPU, separating G1 and G2 decoder blocks into distinct nodes that share only the vpu‑blk‑ctrl power domain.
The patch deletes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro and merges its sole usage into the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro, simplifying the iwlwifi configuration code.
